vulnerability.title: Disable SSH Access via Empty Passwords
vulnerability.description: Disallow SSH login with empty passwords. The default SSH configuration disables logins with empty passwords. The appropriate configuration is used if no value is set for PermitEmptyPasswords. To explicitly disallow SSH login from accounts with empty passwords, add or correct the following line in /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/00-complianceascode-hardening.conf: PermitEmptyPasswords no Any accounts with empty passwords should be disabled immediately, and PAM configuration should prevent users from being able to assign themselves empty passwords.
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: # Remediation is applicable only in certain platforms if rpm --quiet -q kernel-core; then mkdir -p /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d touch /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf chmod 0600 /etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*PermitEmptyPasswords\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config" LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*PermitEmptyPasswords\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d"/*.conf if [ -e "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf" ] ; then LC_ALL=C sed -i "/^\s*PermitEmptyPasswords\s\+/Id" "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf" else touch "/etc/ssh/sshd_config.d/01-complianceascode-reinforce-os-defaults.conf" fi # make sure file has newline at the end sed -i -e '$a\' "/etc/ssh/

triage_decision.reason: This rule is related to password policy, which is considered low-risk and safe to remediate.

previous_attempts.scan_output: Single-rule scan failed for openscap_071 (rule=xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_sshd_disable_empty_passwords). Could not verify.

llm_verdict.message:
The output indicates a failure to verify the remediation of the OpenSCAP finding. The `scan` function returned a failure, indicating that the finding was not successfully remediated. Further investigation and debugging are needed to determine the cause of the failure. 

It's also important to note that the `run_cmd` and `write_file` functions returned an exit code of 255 and a success value of False, indicating that these commands did not execute successfully. The `stderr` output suggests that there was a connection reset, which could be a sign of a network or SSH issue. 

Therefore, the next step would be to investigate the cause of the connection reset and ensure that the commands are executing successfully. Additionally, it may be necessary to repeat the remediation steps and verify the outcome using the `scan` function. 

Unfortunately, the provided tools and functions are not sufficient to resolve the issue, and additional troubleshooting steps are needed to determine the root cause of the problem.

previous_attempts.scan_output: Single-rule scan failed for openscap_071 (rule=xccdf_org.ssgproject.content_rule_sshd_disable_empty_passwords). Could not verify.

llm_verdict.message:
The remediation process failed to verify the fix for the OpenSCAP finding "Disable SSH Access via Empty Passwords". The scan output indicates that the single-rule scan failed and could not verify the fix. This suggests that the remediation steps taken were not effective in addressing the finding. 

To improve the remediation process, it is recommended to revisit the steps taken and ensure that they are accurate and effective. Additionally, it may be helpful to review the OpenSCAP finding and the relevant configuration files to better understand the issue and identify the correct solution. 

In this case, the remediation steps included reading the SSH configuration file, modifying the file to include the "PermitEmptyPasswords no" directive, checking the syntax of the SSH configuration, restarting the SSH service, and verifying the presence of the "PermitEmptyPasswords no" directive in the configuration files. However, these steps did not result in a successful verification of the fix. 

Therefore, further investigation and analysis are needed to identify the root cause of the issue and develop an effective remediation plan.

review_feedback: Remediation failed to correctly set PermitEmptyPasswords=no; multiple commands returned exit code 255, sshd -t failed, and verification indicates the fix was not applied. Suggestions: Use grep to check existing PermitEmptyPasswords lines before appending; Ensure sed edits succeed; handle non‑zero exit codes gracefully; Run sshd -t before restarting the service and abort on failure; Restart sshd only after successful syntax validation; Log modifications and verify final configuration contains PermitEmptyPasswords no

concerns: Remediation script failed with exit code 255 on multiple commands
systemctl status sshd returned error, indicating service may not be running correctly
PermitEmptyPasswords directive may not have been applied across all configuration files
No validation that the resulting configuration satisfies the OpenSCAP rule
Potential risk of incomplete hardening due to script errors
suggested_improvements: Add a syntax check (sshd -t) before restarting the SSH service
Verify that PermitEmptyPasswords=no is present in all relevant config files after modification
Implement idempotent editing to avoid duplicate entries
Use a configuration management tool or ensure atomic updates
Log and handle errors gracefully rather than letting them propagate

previous_review_verdicts.feedback: Remediation failed to correctly set PermitEmptyPasswords=no; multiple commands returned exit code 255, sshd -t failed, and verification indicates the fix was not applied.
concerns: Exit code 255 reported for sed and echo commands indicating failures
sshd -t syntax check failed, preventing a safe restart
Changes may not be applied to all configuration files
No idempotent handling leading to possible duplicate entries
Lack of validation that PermitEmptyPasswords=no exists in all relevant files
suggested_improvements: Use grep to check existing PermitEmptyPasswords lines before appending
Ensure sed edits succeed; handle non‑zero exit codes gracefully
Run sshd -t before restarting the service and abort on failure
Restart sshd only after successful syntax validation
Log modifications and verify final configuration contains PermitEmptyPasswords no
